priorFindingsStatus.note: Verified via `git show 76fddc7` that the it3 fix commit touches ONLY verification-architecture.md's PROP-DX-006 step 4 (15 insertions, 6 deletions; no other file, section, or line changed). The restored text now reads: defi's case, run against today's pre-fix code (before REQ-DX-015's extractErrorMessage() extension exists), SHALL FAIL because extractErrorMessage() currently collapses the stubbed error to the bare string "fetch failed" with no cause/name information; ONCE REQ-DX-015 is applied, defi's case SHALL PASS. REQ-DX-016 is explicitly marked UNRELATED to this gating, with the stated reason matching REQ-DX-011's own classification-order clause verbatim: network_error (item 6) is checked FIRST and independently at step (a), before REQ-DX-016's classifyKnownError extraction is ever consulted at step (b) (confirmed by re-reading REQ-DX-011 lines 141-154 of behavioral-spec.md, which is byte-identical to before — behavioral-spec.md was not touched by this commit, consistent with the corruption having been isolated to the PROP text). dex's case remains unchanged and correctly gated on REQ-DX-017 alone (SEPARATE, INDEPENDENT RED-phase requirement, unaffected by this edit). The closing sentence now reads '(each gated on its OWN correct prerequisite — REQ-DX-015 for defi, REQ-DX-015+REQ-DX-017 for dex)', which is strictly more precise than the pre-it3 wording and introduces no new inconsistency. No other text in PROP-DX-006 (steps 1-3, the RED-phase requirement's surrounding Tier-1/Tier-2 framing, or its cross-references to REQ-DX-011/015/016/017) was altered by this edit.

totalsCheck.note: Recounted directly from behavioral-spec.md (REQ-DX-001..008=8, 010..017=8, 020..024=5, 030..034=5, 040..041=2 => 28, excluding the 5 separately-typed REQ-DX-NG-* non-goals) and verification-architecture.md (PROP-DX-001..014 => 14). Matches the document's own stated totals in both the body text and the traceability summary table; unchanged from iteration-3.
